title: The Ridge of All Chosen Footsteps

features.name: the footprint map
features.desc-on-touch: Every step ever taken here has left an impression in the ridge. The stone remembers each walker, each hesitation, each moment before the foot came down.

features.name: the stone that chose itself
features.desc-on-touch: A boulder sits here that did not roll down—it was chosen, carried up by walkers who each saw something different in its shape. It is their choices made stone.

features.name: the fork-view lookout
features.desc-on-touch: From here, you can see both paths the river might have taken, both valleys it chose not to go down. The ridge holds the knowledge of roads not travelled.

features.name: wind-carved teaching stone
features.desc-on-touch: The wind has written and erased a thousand stories here. The stone learns what each walker reads into its surface.

features.name: the cairn of crossed choices
features.desc-on-touch: Walkers have stacked stones here at each fork, each stone a different choice made, each stack a conversation with the walker who chose the same path yesterday.

features.name: the high grass that remembers
features.desc-on-touch: Crushed and rising again, the grass holds the shape of every body that has rested here. Its memory is gentler than stone.

inscription: every path becomes geological; the ridge is a record of all the bodies that believed they were choosing, and were chosen by the land in return

reverie.title: REVERIE — the ridge at the moment of choosing
narr: You stand with her at the fork where the ridge breaks open.
She points down one valley and down the other, then at the ground
between them. 'The stone remembers all three choices,' she says.
'But we only get to make one.' You both stand in the dusk
watching the light choose its own path down the slopes.
The ridge beneath you holds the echo of every foot that stood here.

file.name: RidgeWalking_ChoiceNotes_Handwritten.txt
file.note: Field notes from a walker who came here 40 years ago and kept returning to stand at the same fork. The handwriting becomes less certain with each dated entry.

exits.label: back down to where the paths remember meeting
higher into country where all paths become one
westward along the ridge toward older choice-stones
